name: Very Berry Cheese Blintzes
recipeIngredient: Egg whites; or 1/2 cup egg substitute
recipeInstructions:
1. In a blender or food processor, combine egg whites (or egg substitute), milk, salt, sugar, wheat germ, flour, and margarine. Blend until smooth.
2. Cover and let stand for 10 minutes to thicken slightly.
3. Heat a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Grease lightly.
4. For each crepe, pour about 1/4 cup batter into the hot skillet. Tilt the pan to coat the bottom evenly with a thin layer of batter.
5. Cook for 45 seconds or until the top looks dry. Turn the crepe and cook for an additional 20 seconds.
6. Stack the cooked crepes between sheets of waxed paper. The crepes can be wrapped securely and frozen up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator.
7. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
8. In a medium bowl, combine ricotta cheese, cream cheese, sour cream, wheat germ, sugar, and vanilla extract. Spoon about 2 tablespoons of filling onto the center of each crepe.
9. Fold the sides of the crepe over the filling, then fold up the ends to form a rectangle.
10. Place the blintzes in the prepared baking dish and brush lightly with melted margarine.
11. Bake for 10 to 15 minutes or until heated through. To serve, top each blintz with fresh berries and additional sour cream. Sprinkle with wheat germ.
Per serving (1 blintz): Approximately 140 calories, 10g protein, 15g carbohydrates, 4g fat, 26% of calories from fat, 0mg cholesterol, 160mg sodium.
